key_insight: WeldMamba predicts future weld-pool geometry from past images, process settings, and sensor signals to enable lookahead segmentation under control delay.
engineering_relevance: This supports closed-loop laser wire-feed welding by giving controllers an early view of keyhole, wire, and molten-pool regions before the actuator responds.
method: Physics-guided spatiotemporal state space network for future semantic layout prediction
Fuses coaxial grayscale history with welding parameters and wire-state electrical signals
Uses motion-aware decoding plus SDF, temporal consistency, distillation, and keyhole-specific losses
evidence_signal: On a 43-sequence laser welding dataset, the method achieved 74.63% mIoU at a 500 ms lookahead.
watch_item: Performance depends on synchronized historical imaging and sensor streams, so timing drift or domain shift in a new welding setup could reduce reliability.
paper_title: Physics-Guided Spatiotemporal State Space Modeling for Lookahead Molten Pool Segmentation in Laser Wire-Feed Welding
